quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 6, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Covalent Bond Counting and Molecular Structure
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Single, Double, and Triple Bonds, Bond Strength, Octet Rule Exceptions, Counting Bonds in Molecules

questions.question: How many electrons are shared in a single covalent bond?
questions.explanation: A single covalent bond involves the sharing of one pair of electrons, which is 2 electrons total.

questions.question: Why is carbon able to form many covalent compounds?
options: It can form four covalent bonds
questions.explanation: Carbon has 4 valence electrons and can form four covalent bonds to achieve a stable octet. This tetravalency allows it to form millions of different compounds, including chains, rings, and branches.

questions.question: Which molecule has only single covalent bonds?
questions.explanation: CH₄ (methane) contains only single covalent bonds (four C–H bonds). CO₂ has double bonds, N₂ has a triple bond, and O₂ has a double bond.

questions.question: Which bond is the strongest?
questions.explanation: Triple bonds are the strongest covalent bonds because they share three pairs of electrons, creating greater electrostatic attraction between the nuclei and shared electrons. Bond strength increases with bond order.

questions.question: Covalent bonds are strongest when:
questions.explanation: Covalent bonds are strongest when more electrons are shared (higher bond order). Triple bonds are stronger than double bonds, which are stronger than single bonds.

questions.question: Which molecule has both single and double covalent bonds?
questions.explanation: CO₂ has both single and double bonds in its resonance structures, but in reality, it has two equivalent double bonds (O=C=O). The question may be testing resonance, but strictly speaking, CO₂ has only double bonds.

questions.question: Which molecule contains only single covalent bonds?
questions.explanation: NH₃ (ammonia) contains only single covalent bonds (three N–H bonds). O₂ has a double bond, CO₂ has double bonds, and N₂ has a triple bond.

questions.question: Which molecule contains two double covalent bonds?
questions.explanation: CO₂ (carbon dioxide) contains two double bonds (O=C=O). O₂ has one double bond, N₂ has one triple bond, and H₂ has one single bond.

questions.question: H₂O has:
questions.explanation: H₂O (water) has two single covalent bonds (O–H bonds). Oxygen shares one pair of electrons with each hydrogen atom.

questions.question: CH₄ contains:
questions.explanation: CH₄ (methane) contains four single covalent bonds (four C–H bonds). Carbon shares one pair of electrons with each of the four hydrogen atoms.

questions.question: According to the Octet rule, SO₂ contains:
options: One double and one single bond
Two single and one double bond
questions.explanation: SO₂ (sulfur dioxide) has resonance structures, but typically it is represented with one double bond (S=O) and one single bond (S–O) with a lone pair on sulfur. Sulfur can expand its octet, so this is an exception to the strict octet rule.

questions.question: C₂H₆ contains:
options: One double and six single bonds
One double and four single bonds
questions.explanation: C₂H₆ (ethane) contains seven single covalent bonds: one C–C bond and six C–H bonds.

questions.question: PCl₅ contains:
options: Three single and one double bond
questions.explanation: PCl₅ (phosphorus pentachloride) contains five single covalent bonds between phosphorus and each chlorine atom. Phosphorus expands its octet using d-orbitals.

questions.question: Which of the following has the strongest covalent bond?
questions.explanation: Diamond has the strongest covalent bonds overall. Each carbon forms four strong C–C single bonds in a giant covalent lattice, making the entire crystal extremely hard and requiring enormous energy to break. N₂ has a strong triple bond but only between two atoms, not a network. Graphite has strong in-plane bonds but weak interlayer forces. H₂O has relatively weak single bonds.

questions.question: XeF₄ contains:
options: No bonds, as Xenon is an inert noble gas
Four single covalent bonds and two lone pairs
Two double, two single bonds and one lone pair
questions.explanation: XeF₄ (xenon tetrafluoride) contains four single covalent bonds between xenon and fluorine atoms, with two lone pairs on xenon. Xenon, though a noble gas, can form compounds under certain conditions by expanding its octet.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered covalent bond counting and molecular structures! 🧪🔗
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review bond counting in molecules with multiple bonds.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on single vs double vs triple bonds and octet rule exceptions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review covalent bond types, bond counting, and molecular structures.
